text: This C4ISRNET article explains that the US, Canada and European allies attributed the 24 February attack on Viasat to Russia and described it as intended to cripple Ukrainian command and control just before the invasion. It reports that the attack disrupted internet service for tens of thousands of people and affected wind farms and users in central Europe. The piece cites statements from US, EU and Estonian officials, including references to Russia’s intelligence directorate, and frames the incident as part of how Russia wages war in cyberspace alongside conventional operations.
